question: Choose the correct word: 'The thaob is made from ________ and is usually black.' (soft cloth produced by a silkworm)
choices: dye
silk
thread
ivory

question: Choose the correct word: 'They also sometimes use other materials such as metal or ________ in making the thaob.' (valuable jewels such as diamonds)
choices: geometric patterns
heritage items
embroidery designs
precious stones

question: The word 'resilience' means ________.
choices: the ability to keep going when life is difficult
a tendency to avoid difficult or stressful situations
the skill of organising your time and tasks efficiently
a method of solving problems by working with others

question: The word 'legislation' refers to ________.
choices: the important ministers who control government policy
the first version of a proposed law before it is passed
the principles and ideas by which a country is governed
the process of making laws

question: Choose the correct preposition: 'I was ________ the impression that the concert started at 8, but it started at 7.'
choices: at
in
by
under

question: Choose the correct sentence using 'at least':
choices: You should watch at least a few episodes before deciding whether you like the series
She arrived at least late to the theatre without any excuse
At least he didn't finish the film before telling us what happened
The show was at least not finished when we arrived

question: Which sentence correctly uses 'in fact'?
choices: She was in fact surprised, so she didn't say anything
He agreed with everyone in fact that the film was terrible
In fact, spoilers can enhance our experience of a story
In fact they were under the impression to enjoy the show

question: Choose the past modal that best completes: 'I _____ attention in class yesterday — I missed the whole explanation.'
choices: can't have been paying
must have been paying
would have been paying
might have been paying

question: Choose the past modal that best completes: 'These pointed wooden swords _____ for practice — they are not sharp enough to be used in real combat.'
choices: must have been used
can't have been used
might have been used
would have been used

question: Choose the past modal that best completes: 'These bronze coins _____ to pay soldiers — they were found near an ancient military site.'
choices: can't have been used
might not have been used
may have been used
would have been used

question: Choose the past modal: 'He must have been wearing this golden mask during burial.' This sentence uses the past modal to express ________.
choices: a negative deduction — the speaker is sure it was not true
a past possibility — the speaker is not sure
a positive deduction — the speaker is pretty sure it was true
an assumption based purely on guesswork

question: Choose the correct past modal to complete: 'The pots on the platform _____ in around 150 BCE, at about the same time as the city of Petra grew up.'
choices: might have to be made
would have been making
will be made
would have been made

question: Choose the correct reduced form: 'As she had studied Mathematics, she was used to making complicated calculations.'
choices: Studied Mathematics, she was used to making complicated calculations
She was used to making complicated calculations, studying Mathematics
Having studied Mathematics, she was used to making complicated calculations
Not having studied Mathematics, she was used to making complicated calculations

question: Choose the correct reduced form of: 'He was feeling nervous about giving his presentation, so he took deep calming breaths.'
choices: Nervous about giving his presentation, he took deep calming breaths
Having felt nervous about his presentation, he took calming breaths
Felt nervous, he took deep calming breaths before the presentation
Feeling nervous about giving his presentation, he took deep calming breaths

question: Choose the correctly reduced form: 'Cameron wanted to do better in life, so he learnt to act.'
choices: Wanting to do better in life, Cameron learnt to act
Having wanted to do better in life, Cameron learnt to act
Cameron learnt to act, wanting to do better in life
Not wanting to do better in life, Cameron learnt to act

question: Choose the correct confused word: 'All's Well That Ends Well is a ________ comedy.' (standard, timeless, iconic)
choices: historical
classic
classical
comical

question: Choose the correct confused word: 'This book is full of ________ advice for drama students.' (extremely useful)
choices: worthless
childlike
invaluable
insipid

question: Choose the correct confused word: 'The final week of rehearsals was ________ — the play was awful on opening night.' (no value)
choices: worthless
invaluable
classic
lively

question: Choose the correct confused word: 'The actor is not very well known, but his ________ acting style wins the audience over completely.' (funny/related to comedy)
choices: historical
comical
classical
comic

question: The adjective 'jarring' describes something that ________.
choices: blends perfectly with its surroundings
produces a pleasant and calming effect
is so beautiful it takes your breath away
is harsh, unpleasant, or difficult to accept

question: The adjective 'distinctive' used to describe a design means ________.
choices: it has a unique quality that makes it easy to recognise
it is poorly made and lacks any identifiable style
it is produced using only the most expensive materials
it is very common and can be found in every market

question: Which of the following is used to MAKE A FINAL DECISION in informal negotiation?
choices: How would you feel about changing the venue?
I don't know if I would fancy that idea
Is everyone happy with that?
Isn't that a bit unprofessional?

question: Which of the following is an example of asking for clarification in informal negotiation?
choices: I could go along with that. Fair enough
I thought perhaps we could do something different
I'm happy with that proposal
What kind of thing were you thinking of?

question: The expression 'I don't want to be awkward, but...' in negotiation is used to ________.
choices: make a strong and direct suggestion about what should be done
politely introduce a disagreement or concern about a suggestion
accept a suggestion and express enthusiasm about it
make a final decision to bring the negotiation to a close

question: According to the excerpt about spoilers, what did the research suggest?
choices: Spoilers always ruin a reading experience completely
Both groups had equally positive reading experiences
Spoilers can enhance our experience of a story
Reading introductions makes stories harder to follow

question: According to the excerpt, why do people enjoy re-reading classic books?
choices: Because they want to find mistakes made by the author
Because they enjoy being surprised by the ending again
Because they want to discover if the story has changed
Because they are free to notice more detail without suspense

question: Which countries share a similar constitutional monarchy system to Jordan?
choices: Germany, France and Egypt
China, Russia and the United States
The United Kingdom, Spain and Japan
Saudi Arabia, Turkey and Morocco

question: The Museum of Parliamentary Life in Jordan was home to the National Assembly from ________.
Districts and subdistricts in Jordan are headed by an official appointed by ________.
choices: the Ministry of the Interior
the House of Deputies
the Prime Minister
the local governor

question: In the poem 'Stopping by Woods on a Snowy Evening', the word 'queer' means ________.
choices: beautiful and worth admiring for a long time
frightening and dangerous in cold weather
strange or unusual
familiar and comforting to the horse

question: In the poem 'Stopping by Woods on a Snowy Evening', which sound is NOT described?
choices: the bells on the horse's harness
the easy wind
the downy flake
the church bells ringing in the village

question: In the poem 'Stopping by Woods on a Snowy Evening', the rhyme scheme in the FIRST three verses follows the pattern ________.
choices: all four lines rhyme with each other
lines 1, 3, and 4 rhyme; line 2 is different
lines 1, 2, and 4 rhyme; line 3 is different
lines 2, 3, and 4 rhyme; line 1 is different

question: The word 'rhyme' in the context of poetry means ________.
choices: the use of the same letter at the beginning of two nearby words
the repetition of the same idea throughout the poem
the use of the same sound at the end of words in a poem
the division of a poem into equal sections or verses

question: To 'give your opinion and make connections between events', you could use the phrase ________.
choices: The plot is quite complex and difficult to follow
The series tells the story of a young boy who grows up in poverty
I would argue that this is an excellent example of modern storytelling
The lead actor's performance draws in the viewer completely

question: The phrase '... perfectly illustrates how/why...' is used to ________.
choices: describe the plot of a story in a neutral and factual way
reject an idea or argue against a particular point of view
ask the reader a question to encourage them to think more deeply
connect the writer's opinion to evidence from the performance

question: Which of the following is an example of a 'feature-length' film?
choices: A five-minute short comedy sketch
A 90-minute full cinema film
A single episode of a TV drama
A collection of short videos posted online

question: The word 'set' in the context of theatre or film refers to ________.
choices: the written text spoken by the actors on stage
the scenery and physical environment where the action takes place
the list of actors who appear in all scenes of the production
the final scene where the main conflict is resolved

question: The word 'scene' in the context of theatre or film refers to ________.
choices: a part of a play or film set in one particular place or time
a collection of episodes that form a complete TV series
the physical surroundings and background on the stage
a joke told by a comedian during a stand-up performance

question: Which of the following is a synonym for 'performance' to avoid repetition?
choices: plot
episode
acting
dialogue
